sessions.intent: Group trace viewer sidebar by .goal file path; add build datetime; nest public/private goals with full history.

before.assumptions: Flat trace list in sidebar; no file grouping; no build datetime; traces carry {id, goal, timestamp, pass1, detailPasses} with no path or visibility.
before.risk: Rebuilding system/builder/BuildGoal.goal to add path/visibility may hit LLM drift on the builder itself.

sessions.plan: Three surgical changes: (1) BuildGoal.goal: add %goal.Path% and %goal.Visibility% to the %trace% JSON. (2) server.py: cap /api/traces at 20 newest, filter tick-prefixed filenames. (3) index.html: replace flat #goal-list with file -> goal -> trace tree; public first, private second; selection per trace, expansion state persisted across 3s polls.

actions.context: Added path and visibility fields to the %trace% JSON object. Rebuilding the builder itself drifted other steps (description rewritten, throw parameter shape changed); reverted the .pr and per Ingi hand-edited only the one set-trace step to match the new .goal.
Filter to tick-prefixed filenames (skip legacy Start.json, Nuget.json), reverse-sort (tick prefix is chronological), cap at 20. Disk storage untouched.

Flat list -> file/goal/trace tree. New CSS for tree rows. buildFileTree() groups entries by path then by goal name, splits public/private, sorts traces newest-first per goal, sinks unknown path to bottom. Selection state is per trace, expansion state preserved across polls.
Two-goal smoke case (Start public + Hello2 private) to verify path/visibility flow through the trace pipeline. Trace files accumulate on disk as Ingi requested.

after.status: ready for Ingi to pull and continue with BuildGoal.llm edits.
after.health: Viewer tree renders. Start.goal builds cleanly and produces two traces with path=/Start.goal, visibility=public/private.
after.notes: Hand-edited buildgoal.pr is a known deviation; replacing it requires a clean builder rebuild without LLM drift. That is a BuildGoal.llm prompt issue, not a runtime issue.

sessions.intent: Restructure action catalog summary.md: fix type names, add descriptions, prune examples, group by module.

before.assumptions: Baseline: 427 lines, 28487 bytes, 76 e.g. lines, 2 leaked concurrentdictionary occurrences. Modules: 24+, actions: 117.
before.risk: Phase 4 (summary.md template) depends on Scriban/DotLiquid capabilities — may need fallback if templating doesn't support grouping.

sessions.plan: Phase 1: TypeMapping.cs type fixes. Phase 2: Add descriptions to attributes/action/modules. Phase 3: Prune/rewrite examples. Phase 4: Rewrite summary.md template. Phase 5: Measure.

sessions.intent: Deliver all catalog phases: TypeMapping normalization, module/action descriptions, example pruning+rewrite to formal syntax, template verification.

before.assumptions: Phases 1, 2a-2e already done. Phase 3 (example pruning/rewrite to formal pipe syntax) is the remaining work. Fluid IgnoreCasing=true means PascalCase properties work in template.
before.risk: 36 examples across multiple modules need individual evaluation — some drop, some rewrite to formal syntax.

sessions.plan: Phase 3 is the remaining work. Audit: Phases 1, 2a-2e, 4 already implemented. Phase 3: evaluate all 36 examples; drop 18 that restate the signature; keep/rewrite 18 to formal pipe syntax (module.action Param([type] value) | variable.set Name([string] %var%), Value([object] %__data__%)).

actions.context: Phase 3: drop 18 examples that restate signature (assert.*, condition.else, error.throw, event.remove, file.copy/delete/move/save, output.write); rewrite 18 keepers to formal pipe syntax. Build stayed green.

after.health: 0 errors, build green. 36 examples → 18, all keepers in formal module.action pipe syntax.
after.notes: Phases 1+2 were pre-committed. Phase 3 commit: 7c0beeec. Phase 4 requires no code change (Fluid IgnoreCasing=true).

sessions.intent: Three-state prior-build hints (@known / @hint / none) + keep:true LLM response shortcut + trace source tag + viewer chips. Stop the LLM from hallucinating '<= null' operator semantics and from re-emitting mappings on every rebuild.

before.assumptions: Template appends `<= null` on fresh steps and `<= actions` on rebuilds. Goal.MergeFrom matches steps by exact text only — drift breaks carry-over. Scheme requires guidance/formal/actions. Viewer has no provenance indicator.
before.risk: Builder's own .pr files reference the llm.query scheme; tightening scheme requires hand-edits to BuildGoal.pr and ApplyStep.pr to survive the migration.

sessions.plan: 1) C# Step.PriorText (transient) + two-pass Goal.MergeFrom (exact then positional). 2) Template emits @known / @hint / bare based on PriorText vs Text. 3) BuildGoal.llm teaches the three markers + keep:true shortcut. 4) Scheme in BuildGoal.goal loosens guidance/formal/level/confidence, adds keep?:bool; ApplyStep skips merge when keep; validator guards keep:true against missing prior or conflicting actions. 5) New builder.enrichResponse action: backfill formal/actions for keep:true steps from prior .pr, tag each step with source:new|known|hint. 6) Hand-edit builder .pr where scheme/apply changed, rebuild. 7) Viewer adds per-step chip new/known/hint + kept overlay. Full plan at .bot/runtime2-build-trace-viewer/coder/v5/plan.md.

sessions.intent: Fix condition.if + loop.foreach error swallowing (silent Handled suppression) and simplify formal/FormatValue to scalar-or-JSON so Fluid wrapper class-names stop leaking into prompts.

before.assumptions: v5 left builder unable to self-rebuild ApplyStep.goal. Fluid wrapper string 'Fluid.Values.ObjectDictionaryFluidIndexable`1[System.Object]' lands in goal.call.Name. Previous handoff blamed list<action> merge (disproved), then Fluid rendering. 3-whys: rendering bug in formal filter is immediate cause; condition.if + loop.foreach swallow the 404 GoalNotFound error so the bug never surfaced loudly.
before.risk: Error-propagation fix may surface previously-silent bugs in other goals/tests. Acceptable per Ingi's direction — fix root cause, don't mask.

sessions.plan:
# v6 — Plan: Fix silent-error swallowing + formal rendering of complex values

## Background

v5 left the builder unable to self-rebuild `/builder/ApplyStep.goal`. Symptom:
ApplyBuiltStep step 0 saved with 0 actions. The original handoff blamed a
list<action> merge bug, then corrected to "Fluid-wrapper type-string
ends up in goal.call.Name". Both are symptoms.

The 3-whys trace (this session):

1. **Why does step 0 stay empty?** Apply chain never runs — `goal.call` has
   garbage `Name` → goal lookup fails.
2. **Why is `goal.call.Name` garbage?** LLM echoed it back from the prompt's
   `@known:` section. On save, `TypeMapping` line 533 accepts any string as a
   goal name.
3. **Why does the prompt contain garbage?** `FormatFormalValue` in
   `FluidProvider.cs` calls `input.ToObjectValue()` on a `Fluid.Values.DictionaryValue`
   — which returns an `ObjectDictionaryFluidIndexable<object>` wrapper, **not**
   the underlying dict. Verified empirically: the wrapper is not `IDictionary`,
   falls through to `v.ToString()` → Fluid class-name leaks into the prompt.

**But why didn't this scream the first time?** Because when the garbage
goal.call executes, `GoalCall.GetGoalAsync` DOES return a proper 404
`GoalNotFound` error. The error gets **swallowed** by `loop/foreach.cs:50`:

```csharp
if (!result.Success && !result.Handled) return result;
```

`condition.if` correctly stamps `Handled=true` on its orchestrated result
(this is needed so `Step.RunAsync` doesn't re-iterate the branch actions
it already ran). But `loop.foreach` conflates `Handled` with "error is fine"
and silently continues. Every `foreach, call ApplyStep` run loops through
all items reporting success while every inner call errored.

This is the priority. Fixing it means any future version of this class of
bug gets caught on the first build.

## Scope

Three fixes, in order:

### 1. Propagate errors from `loop.foreach` body

`PLang/App/modules/loop/foreach.cs:50`

```csharp
- if (!result.Success && !result.Handled) return result;
+ if (!result.Success) return result;
```

`Handled` stays for its real purpose (telling `Step.RunAsync` "don't iterate
remaining step actions") but never suppresses error propagation.

### 2. Simplify `formal` filter + `FormatValue` — scalar-or-JSON

Both `FluidProvider.FormatFormalValue` (`PLang/App/modules/ui/providers/FluidProvider.cs:128`)
and `DefaultBuilderProvider.FormatValue` (`PLang/App/modules/builder/providers/DefaultBuilderProvider.cs:414`)
share the same bug pattern: they fall through to `v.ToString()` for anything
not string/bool/IDict/IList.

New rule, both places:

```
null                  → "null"
string starts with %  → bare
string with space/,   → "quoted"
string                → bare
bool                  → true/false
IConvertible (numbers, enums with ToString)
                      → v.ToString()
anything else         → JsonSerializer.Serialize(v)
```

The key change: the final fallback becomes JSON serialize, not `v.ToString()`.
No Fluid wrappers, no POCO type-names, no surprises. Goal.call values,
dict-typed params, any nested structure — all render as JSON.

For the `formal` filter specifically: continue to call `input.ToObjectValue()`
(Fluid's unwrapping API), but the scalar-vs-JSON rule above handles the
case where ToObjectValue returns a wrapper rather than the raw dict.

### 3. Clear the poisoned .pr and rebuild

`system/builder/.build/applystep.pr` has `Fluid.Values...` strings in its
`goal.call` params. With #1 and #2 in place, the next rebuild will:
- Render `p.Value | formal` correctly (JSON → `{"name":"ApplyKeptStep","parameters":[]}`)
- LLM sees correct goal name in `@known`, echoes it back
- TypeMapping creates `GoalCall { Name = "ApplyKeptStep" }`
- Self-rebuild works

But the stored .pr is already poisoned — the first rebuild still sends
`Fluid.Values...` via `@known:` unless the .pr is fixed first. Manually
edit `applystep.pr` to replace the two bad `value: {...}` blobs with
`value: "ApplyKeptStep"` / `value: "ApplyBuiltStep"`. Then rebuild.

## Unit tests

### New: `PLang.Tests/App/Modules/loop/ForeachErrorPropagationTests.cs`

Three tests for the regression — all hit the real path, no mocks:

1. **`Foreach_BodyActionReturnsError_PropagatesError`** — foreach over 3 items,
   body is `goal.call NonExistentGoal`. Assert the overall result is `!Success`,
   `Error.Key == "GoalNotFound"`, and only the first iteration ran (not 3).
2. **`Foreach_BodyActionReturnsErrorWithHandled_StillPropagates`** — the exact
   scenario from this bug: body is a `condition.if + goal.call NonExistentGoal`
   chain. condition.if stamps `Handled=true` on the error. Assert error still
   propagates from the outer foreach.
3. **`Foreach_BodyActionSucceeds_CompletesAllIterations`** — sanity check that
   the fix didn't break the happy path.

### New: `PLang.Tests/App/Modules/condition/IfErrorOrchestrationTests.cs`

Two tests:

1. **`If_OrchestratedBranchActionErrors_ReturnsError`** — step with
   `[condition.if (true), goal.call NonExistentGoal]`. Call `step.RunAsync()`.
   Assert `!Success`, `Error.Key == "GoalNotFound"`.
2. **`If_OrchestratedResult_IsMarkedHandled_OnSuccess`** — happy path: assert
   successful orchestration sets `Handled=true` (so Step.RunAsync breaks).
   This pins the existing correct behavior so it doesn't regress.

### PLang-level test: `tests/v0.2/loop/ForeachErrorPropagation.test.goal`

End-to-end check with a real goal file — foreach over items, inner call
to a missing goal. Assert the build terminates with the 404 error, not
a silent success. This catches any future regression in the
builder→LLM→.pr→runtime chain that re-introduces silent swallowing.

## Why not just `return await Orchestrate(...)` in condition.if?

Answered: because `Step.RunAsync` iterates `Step.Actions` one-by-one
(`Step/this.cs:127-132`). `Orchestrate` already runs all branch actions.
Without `Handled=true`, `Step.RunAsync` continues to the next sibling and
re-executes everything `Orchestrate` already ran. The `Handled` flag is
condition.if's signal to Step: "I consumed the siblings, don't iterate
them again."

`loop.foreach.cs:57` sets the same flag for the same reason (its body is
"remaining step actions"). Both writes are correct. The **read** in
`loop.foreach.cs:50` is the only misuse.

## All Handled sites audited

**Writes (3, all legitimate):**
- `condition/if.cs:54` — orchestrated branch consumed siblings
- `loop/foreach.cs:57` — foreach body consumed siblings
- `Events/Lifecycle/Bindings/Binding/this.cs:60` — event override replacing action

**Reads (4 correct, 1 buggy):**
- `Step/this.cs:122, 131` — correct: break step loop
- `Goal/this.cs:280` — correct: event override short-circuits goal
- `Bindings/this.cs:39` — correct: stop running more bindings
- `loop/foreach.cs:50` — **BUG** (conflates Handled with "error is fine")

## Out of scope

- `keep:true` integrity check (`GoalCall.Name` validation). Once errors
  propagate correctly (fix #1), a bad Name causes a 404 on the very next
  rebuild. No additional validation layer needed — the runtime is enforcing
  "goal exists" already. Drop the earlier #5 suggestion.
- Any further builder/prompt cleanup. One fix at a time.
- The `tests/simple/.build/traces/` and `os/.build/traces/` dirty files in
  the working tree — unrelated to this bug, leave alone.

## Order of execution

1. Write both C# test files — they should FAIL against current code, proving
   the bug exists.
2. Apply fix #1 (foreach one-liner). Re-run tests — should pass.
3. Apply fix #2 (formal filter + FormatValue scalar-or-JSON).
4. Fix the poisoned applystep.pr manually (fix #3).
5. Full rebuild from `/workspace/plang/system`. Verify ApplyBuiltStep step 0
   now has 2 actions and builder self-rebuild is idempotent.
6. Write v6/summary.md, commit (including .bot/), generate changes.patch,
   push.

## Risks

- Fix #1 (foreach error propagation) might surface existing "silently passing"
  tests/builds that were relying on the bug. Run the full test suite
  (`dotnet run --project PLang.Tests`) and `plang build` from `/workspace/plang`
  before and after; compare. Any test that breaks probably had a real bug
  masked — investigate, don't paper over.
- Fix #2's JSON fallback means some existing prompts that rendered
  POCOs-via-ToString will switch to JSON. Acceptable — JSON is richer and
  more useful for the LLM.
- Manual .pr edit (fix #3) is a one-shot migration. After rebuild, the
  correct value sticks.

actions.context: 5 regression tests. ForeachErrorPropagation#2 reproduces the exact bug (body action returns Handled=true with Success=false; outer foreach must propagate, not swallow). Verified red against unfixed code, green after fix. IfErrorOrchestration pins Step-level error-propagation contract + Handled=true-on-success invariant so future refactors can't silently drop them.

One-line fix at line 50: drop `&& !result.Handled` from body-iteration error check. Errors always propagate; Handled keeps its step-control meaning (informing Step.RunAsync to stop iterating remaining step actions). Full audit of 7 Handled sites confirmed this was the only misuse. All other reads (Step/this.cs, Goal/this.cs, Bindings/this.cs) correctly use Handled for control flow only; all three writes (condition/if, loop/foreach, event bindings) legitimately signal 'siblings consumed'.

Scalar-or-JSON rule for both formal value renderers. FluidProvider adds UnwrapFluid helper that recursively walks IFluidIndexable.Keys + TryGetValue back to plain CLR dicts/lists (empirically verified — Fluid.DictionaryValue.ToObjectValue returns ObjectDictionaryFluidIndexable wrapper, not the raw dict, so naive IDictionary check misses). DefaultBuilderProvider sees raw CLR objects (no Fluid wrapping) so just needed the IConvertible-or-JSON rule. Eliminates the v.ToString() fallback that leaked class names.

Hand-edited two goal.call params that had Name='Fluid.Values.ObjectDictionaryFluidIndexable`1[System.Object]' stuck from prior poisoned rebuilds. After edit, running plang build with fixes applied re-serializes cleanly. Verified both prior-state (silent 'success') and post-fix (loud ValidationErrors) by stashing code and rebuilding.
v6 plan doc with full 3-whys trace + scope. v6 summary covers what changed + verification + the downstream LLM-drift issue left for v7. Updated bot-root summary with one-paragraph v6 hook.

after.health: tests green (2282/2284; 2 pre-existing failures unrelated). All 5 new tests pass. The downstream LLM-drift issue (keep:true + actions in response) is now LOUDLY surfaced — v7 investigation. ApplyStep.goal self-rebuild is partially unblocked: Apply chain runs correctly; secondary LLM-drift blocks full build success.
after.notes: Fix was simpler than the v5 handoff implied. Silent error was load-bearing — once fixed, the real LLM bug surfaces and becomes tractable.

sessions.intent: Unblock ApplyStep self-rebuild by making validateResponse permissive for keep:true+actions (enrichResponse already overwrites) + tighten BuildGoal prompt to reduce LLM drift.

before.assumptions: v6 fixed error-propagation and Fluid rendering. Apply chain runs, surfaces real downstream bug: LLM emits keep:true + actions:[...] simultaneously, validateResponse.cs:88-89 rejects it. But enrichResponse overwrites actions from prior anyway when keep:true — so the check is redundant guardrail blocking valid builds.
before.risk: Dropping a validation check could mask real prompt issues. Mitigated by: (1) keeping the remaining check (keep:true + empty prior) which is a real pipeline break, (2) tightening the prompt so drift happens less often, (3) new tests pin both accepted and rejected cases.

sessions.plan: Plan was given inline in the chat (not as plan.md). Summary: Fix 1: delete validateResponse.cs lines 88-89 (keep+actions error). Keep 90-92 (keep+empty-prior). Fix 2: add explicit prompt rule in system/builder/llm/BuildGoal.llm — when keep:true, omit actions key. Tests: 2 cases in ValidateResponseTests (permissive accept, still-error for empty prior). Verify: rebuild /builder/ApplyStep.goal twice for idempotency.

actions.context: Added 3 tests for the keep:true matrix: happy path (keep+no-actions+prior-has-actions), v7 fix pin (keep+actions+prior-has-actions = accepted), remaining guardrail (keep+empty-prior = error). v7 fix pin fails red before, passes green after.

Dropped lines 88-89 (keep:true + actions error). Kept empty-prior check. enrichResponse already overwrites actions from prior when keep:true, so the validation was redundant guardrail blocking LLM drift from being silently corrected.
Restructured @known response contract into FOR/EMIT/OMIT/DO-NOT pseudo-code block. Prose rules were being skimmed by the LLM. Defense-in-depth — runtime now permissive, prompt tighter.
Regenerated by successful rebuild. applystep.pr now has correct structure — ApplyBuiltStep step 0 has 1 action (was 0 in v5 — the original chase target is resolved).
v7 summary with verification, code example, known-caveat section on the separate LLM step-count drift. Bot-root summary updated with one paragraph.

after.health: All 3 new v7 tests green. Full suite 2285/2287 (2 same pre-existing unrelated failures). ApplyStep.goal self-rebuild now succeeds. v5 origin bug (ApplyBuiltStep step 0 with 0 actions) resolved end-to-end.
after.notes: Pattern recurred: when two systems (validator + enricher) disagreed about the same data, the clean answer was to have the validator defer. enrichResponse is authoritative for keep:true actions; the validator was second-guessing.
